Landing.heroSubtitle: Build pages, databases, and Kanban boards that work the way you actually think.
Landing.heroNote: No credit card required · Open source
Landing.featuresSubtitle: Write, plan, and track without juggling five different tools.
Landing.feature1Desc: A block editor that stays out of the way. Headings, task lists, tables, code blocks. Just start typing.
Landing.feature2Desc: Text, numbers, dates, dropdowns. Build the columns that actually make sense for your project.
Landing.feature3Desc: Drag cards between columns, track progress, ship things. Your database, now as a board.
Landing.feature4Desc: Switch to calendar and see what's due this week or this month. No setup needed.
Landing.feature5Desc: Ships in English, Turkish, Hindi, Spanish, French, and German. Switch anytime from the sidebar.
Landing.feature6Desc: Pages inside pages, databases inside a doc. Nest things as deep as you want.
Landing.footerTagline: A workspace that gets out of your way.
Landing.bridgeHeroSubhead: Pages, databases, kanban. With MCP built in, so your AI works the same surface your team does.
Landing.bridgeWhyPart2: , working the same workspace as your team.
Landing.bridgeInsideBody: Flip between table, board, and calendar. Your data, not the tool’s.
Landing.bridgeInsideTabBoardSub: Group by status, drag cards between columns
Landing.bridgeInsideTabCalendarSub: See what’s due, drag to reschedule
Landing.bridgeInsidePagesH3: Docs that live next to your data
Landing.bridgeInsidePagesBody: Standalone pages with full markdown support. Nest them inside databases or keep them at the workspace root.
Landing.bridgeInsideCallout2: Pages, databases, boards, and calendars — side by side in the same sidebar.
Landing.bridgeIntBody: Add Remnus to Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, ChatGPT, Continue, or Antigravity in under 60 seconds. No new accounts, no extra config.
Landing.bridgeIntClaudeDesc: Full MCP support via Desktop app or API. Reads and writes to your workspace with the same scope your token carries.
Landing.bridgeIntCursorDesc: Add as an MCP server in Cursor settings. Pages, databases, and board state — all in context while you code.
Landing.bridgeIntWindsurfDesc: Cascade reads your workspace items to plan and writes back when it’s done. No copy-paste.
Landing.bridgeIntChatgptDesc: Connect via remote HTTP MCP with OAuth. Full read and write scope supported — setup requires an OAuth app in your ChatGPT settings.
Landing.bridgeIntContinueDesc: Lightweight VS Code extension that plugs into any model. Remnus gives it workspace context.
Landing.bridgeIntAntigravityDesc: Antigravity’s agent can call Remnus tools natively via MCP. Reads, creates, and updates pages. No plugin install.
Landing.bridgeIntFootnote: Any MCP-compatible client can connect to Remnus — if your tool supports remote HTTP MCP, you’re already set.
Landing.bridgeSetupModalSubtitle: Generate a token in workspace settings and add it to your client config. Detailed per-client docs are on the way.
Landing.bridgeSetupModalS1Body: Click the ⚙ gear icon next to your workspace name in the sidebar, then open the MCP tab.
Landing.bridgeSetupModalS2Body: Give it a name and choose a scope — Read for context-only agents, Write to let the AI create or edit pages. The full token is shown once; copy it immediately.
Landing.bridgeSetupModalS3Body: Point your client at the endpoint below and pass the token as the Authorization header. That’s it.
Landing.bridgeSetupModalDocsNote: Per-client config examples (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity…) are coming in the documentation.
Landing.bridgeSetup1Body: Create a workspace in seconds. No credit card, no configuration needed.
Landing.bridgeSetup2Body: Open Workspace Settings → MCP. Set a name and choose read or write scope. Revoke anytime.
Landing.bridgeSetup3Body: Add the URL and token to your MCP client config. Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, ChatGPT and more work out of the box.
Landing.bridgeToolsSubhead: Anything you can do in the UI, your AI can do too. Same auth, same scopes, same audit log.
Landing.bridgeToolsFooterText: Read tokens unlock 5 read tools. Write scope adds 7 more: create_page, update_page, bulk_update, delete_page, move_item, create_database, and update_database_schema.
Landing.bridgeResourcesSubhead: Context is everything. Let your AI agent read pages, databases, and audit logs natively.
Landing.bridgeResourcesDescWorkspace: Workspace database schemas and structure layout.
Landing.bridgeResourcesDescPage: Markdown content & properties for pages and database rows (returns recent 20 pages on list).
Landing.bridgeResourcesDescDatabase: Dynamic column schema definitions of a specific database.
Landing.bridgeResourcesDescAuditLog: Recent 50 agent activity and tool call logs.
Landing.bridgePromptsDescSummarizePage: Summarize any page or database row in bullet, paragraph, or TL;DR format.
Landing.bridgePromptsDescWeeklyReport: Generate a grouped status report (Done / In Progress / Blocked) from a task database.
Landing.bridgePromptsDescKanbanTriage: Identify blockers, reprioritize cards, and surface the next 3 actions.
Landing.bridgePromptsDescExtractTasks: Extract actionable tasks from a page as a markdown checklist with owner and deadline.
Landing.bridgePromptsDescSearchCreate: Search for similar content and suggest a non-duplicate outline for a new page.
Landing.bridgePricingSelfSub: Self-host Remnus on your own infrastructure.
Landing.bridgePricingStartupSub: For growing teams. Hosted and ready to go.
Landing.bridgePricingProSub: For teams that move fast. No limits.
Landing.bridgeClosingSub: Sign up, create a workspace, mint a token, and plug it into Claude, Cursor, or Windsurf. That’s it.
Landing.bridgeFooterTagline: The workspace your AI can work in too.
Landing.bridgeFooterCopyright: © {year} Remnus. Open source. Developed by Akatron.net.
Landing.bridgePricingFreeSub: Try Remnus hosted. No credit card required.

Pricing.title: Pricing
Pricing.subtitle: Free to start. Always will be.
Pricing.freeDesc: Host it yourself and use every feature. No trial, no limit, no catch.
Pricing.proDesc: We host it, you use it. Real-time collaboration included.
Pricing.proNote: Working on it. Star the repo if you want to follow along.

Contact.title: Say hi
Contact.subtitle: Found a bug? Have an idea? Just want to say hi? We're around.
Contact.githubDesc: File bugs, ask questions, open PRs
Contact.emailDesc: For things that don't fit in a GitHub issue
Contact.responseNote: We usually get back within a day or two on GitHub.

Download.title: Download Remnus
Download.subtitle: The native desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Your whole cloud workspace in its own window.
Download.yourSystemBadge: We picked this for your system
Download.latestNote: These links always point to the latest release.
Download.aboutBody: Remnus Desktop is the native app version of your Remnus cloud workspace, built with Tauri. It runs in its own window using a lightweight system WebView instead of a heavy bundled browser, so it stays fast and small. On top of the full web experience it adds desktop conveniences like a system tray, global shortcuts, native notifications, and close-to-tray. Everything stays in sync with the web and mobile apps, so your pages, databases, and workspaces are always up to date across every device.
Download.reqWindowsBody: Windows 10 or later (64-bit). Uses the built-in WebView2 runtime, which ships with current versions of Windows.
Download.reqMacBody: macOS 11 Big Sur or later. Both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs are supported through separate downloads.
Download.reqLinuxBody: A modern 64-bit distribution with WebKitGTK 4.1. The AppImage runs almost anywhere; the .deb targets Debian and Ubuntu.
Download.installWindowsBody: Run the installer and follow the prompts. If Windows SmartScreen appears, choose “More info” then “Run anyway” — the build is not yet code-signed.
Download.installMacBody: Open the .dmg and drag Remnus into your Applications folder. On first launch, right-click the app and choose “Open” to bypass the Gatekeeper warning for unsigned apps.
Download.installLinuxBody: For the AppImage, make it executable (chmod +x Remnus-*.AppImage) and run it directly. For Debian or Ubuntu, install the package with sudo apt install ./Remnus-linux-amd64.deb.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: At Remnus, we maintain the highest standards of commitment to your privacy and data security. This privacy policy outlines how your data is collected, securely stored, and how it integrates with AI clients via MCP (Model Context Protocol).
Privacy.sec1Body: All workspaces, pages, and database contents you create are stored securely in our encrypted cloud databases. Authentication via third-party providers (Google, GitHub, etc.) only processes basic profile information (name, email). Your data is never sold or shared under any circumstances.
Privacy.sec2Body: The access tokens (MCP Tokens) you generate grant AI agents permission to access your workspace strictly within the scopes you define (Read-only or Read & Write). All operations executed by AI agents are permanently logged in system audit trails.
Privacy.sec3Body: Your data is protected using industry-standard encryption protocols and secure session management. Password credentials and sensitive authentication tokens are guarded using strong cryptographic hashing algorithms.
Privacy.sec4Body: For any official inquiries or questions regarding this privacy policy and our data protection practices, please contact us at info@akatron.net.

Security.title: Security & Authentication
Security.intro: Remnus is built with security as a first-class concern. This page documents how we authenticate users and AI agents, what we store, and how you stay in control.
Security.loginBody: Remnus uses OAuth 2.0 with Google and GitHub as identity providers — we never store passwords. When you click "Sign in with Google", your credentials go directly to Google's servers. We only receive a verified email address and profile name. Sessions are managed via secure, httpOnly cookies using Auth.js v5.
Security.patBody: AI agents (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) authenticate to the MCP API using Personal Access Tokens (PATs). Tokens are prefixed rmns_ and the secret portion is stored only as a bcrypt hash — we cannot recover the plaintext. Tokens support read or write scope, optional expiry, and can be revoked at any time from Workspace Settings → Tokens.
Security.oauthBody: Remnus supports the MCP OAuth 2.1 standard (RFC 9728). Compatible clients (Claude Desktop, etc.) open a browser login flow instead of requiring you to paste a token. Access tokens expire after 1 hour; refresh tokens rotate on every use and expire after 30 days. The PKCE S256 challenge prevents authorization code interception. Clients register dynamically via RFC 7591.
Security.scopesBody: Every token — whether a PAT or OAuth — is locked to a single workspace and carries one of two scopes. Read scope allows listing pages, querying databases, and searching content. Write scope additionally allows creating, editing, and deleting pages and database rows. No token can ever access workspaces it was not explicitly granted.
Security.auditBody: Every MCP tool call is recorded in an immutable audit log (agent_activity table) with the tool name, status, timestamp, and token identifier. The last 60 entries are visible in the Workspace Settings → Tokens panel. Audit logs are retained for 7 days on the free plan and 90 days on Pro.
Security.disclosureBody: If you discover a security vulnerability, please email security@remnus.com with a description and reproduction steps. We aim to respond within 48 hours and will credit researchers with their consent. Please do not publicly disclose issues until a fix has been released.
